signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBA Hike
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.092 — RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% provides a +360bp carry advantage, further bolstered by the RBA's 25bps hike to 4.35% on May 5. AUD 1W outperformance reflects hawkish divergence as Westpac flags upside inflation risks. JPY remains the G8 laggard as the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains USD/JPY at 157.801, keeping intervention risk secondary to carry dominance while VIX remains at 17.4 (normal volatility).

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Jobs Beat
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (specs short) vs RBNZ hike bias (market pricing ↑ Hike for May 27). Q1 unemployment fell to 5.3%, beating the 5.4% forecast and providing fundamental support for the RBNZ's hawkish stance. This positioning-policy divergence creates significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ confirms the hike bias; NZD/USD at 0.5929 testing recent highs.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7235 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 86% short. Institutional and retail positioning are in extreme divergence following the RBA hike to 4.35%. Retail is aggressively fading the move, creating a capitulation risk that supports further upside extension toward 0.7300 as long as the -60bp AUD/USD carry premium persists.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.718 vs norm of -0.149 (z=+1.99σ). The pair is decoupling from historical equity drivers, instead tracking the 175bp Fed-ECB carry disadvantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%). Despite the 1D gain to 1.1721, EUR/USD upside remains structurally limited by the carry deficit and a net short COT position of -18,293 contracts.
